event_blocks.summary: The Emirates FA Cup final commences at Wembley Stadium with an electrifying atmosphere, as the trophy is presented amidst smoke and stadium lights, with commentators Derek Rae and Stewart Robson welcoming viewers. Manchester City, in light blue, and Manchester United, in red, emerge onto the pitch, their starting lineups and formations (Manchester City 4-3-3 with Ederson, De Bruyne, Rodri, Haaland; Manchester United 4-2-3-1 with De Gea, Fernandes, Rashford, Antony, Weghorst) displayed on screen. The match begins with the scoreboard at 0-0, and after an initial pass from Mahrez, Manchester City's Erling Haaland scores in the 4th minute, breaking the deadlock and putting Manchester City ahead 1-0. The goal, described as a "lovely goal" where Haaland "made the finish look easy" from a "decent delivery," is followed by a FIFA 23 logo and a replay showing the Manchester United goalkeeper diving in vain. The crowd erupts in celebration, and the commentators question Manchester United's ability to respond after being "shaken up." Gameplay continues with players like De Bruyne, Fernandes, Haaland, and Anfre involved, amidst continuous crowd chants, kicking sounds, and pitch-side advertising for '#EmiratesFACup' and 'FIFA SOUND' under bright stadium lights.

raw_segments: The segment opens dramatically with a close-up of the Emirates FA Cup trophy, shrouded in smoke and illuminated by stadium lights, as a male commentator with a British accent declares it a "special occasion" to be cherished, questioning "Who will go down in history as the winners of the FA Cup?" Orchestral music builds intensity as the scene transitions to the exterior of Wembley Stadium, where excited fans in blue and red jerseys gather, with the stadium's large digital screen displaying 'WEMBLEY STADIUM' and FA Cup branding. Inside, the atmosphere intensifies with an upbeat electronic sports anthem as players from Manchester City, in light blue jerseys, and Manchester United, in red training jackets, walk onto the pitch, flanked by officials and the referee, who holds the official match ball. The commentator announces, "It's Manchester City, they face Manchester United, and it's live on EA TV." The teams line up for the pre-match ceremony, with Manchester City's Haaland (number 9) and Grealish (number 10) clearly visible. The scoreboard shows 0-0 at kickoff, and the commentator, Derek Rae, welcomes viewers to Wembley, joined by Stewart Robson, as they approach kickoff for what promises to be a "spectacular FA Cup final." Stewart Robson, with a nostalgic tone, reflects on the FA Cup final as the day "every young footballer dreamt of," playing in front of "100,000 fans at Wembley," a moment that "still brings a tingle to my spine." The massive crowd, a sea of red and blue, fills the stadium, their cheers audible, creating an electric ambiance as the players prepare for the game.

The segment opens with a close-up of Manchester City players, including Haaland (number 9) with his distinctive high ponytail, Grealish (number 10), and Mahrez (number 26), walking onto the pitch in their light blue jerseys with white shorts and blue socks. An announcer's voice introduces players like "De Bruyne, number 8," "Ilkay Gundogan," and "Riyad Mahrez, number 26," as the stadium crowd roars in anticipation. The scene transitions to a wide aerial view of Wembley Stadium, a massive, modern structure filled with fans predominantly in red and blue, with 'Emirates FA Cup' branding visible on digital screens and the 'EA Sports Live' overlay in the top right corner. A commentator then details Manchester City's starting eleven, mentioning "Ederson between the posts," "Kevin De Bruyne plays alongside Rodri in the center of midfield," and "Erling Haaland" as the main threat, aligning with the on-screen graphic displaying their lineup (including John Stones and manager Pep Guardiola) and a 4-3-3 tactical formation. The crowd's chanting and singing become more prominent. The camera then shifts to Manchester United players in red training jackets with white accents and white shorts, walking in formation, led by the referee in a bright lime green jersey holding the official match ball, which features geometric blue and yellow patterns and 'Emirates FA Cup' branding. A close-up of the goal net precedes a graphic showing Manchester United's starting eleven (including David de Gea, Bruno Fernandes, and manager Erik ten Hag) and a 4-2-3-1 formation, as the commentator announces "David De Gea begins in goal," "Marcus Rashford starts with Antony out wide," and "Wout Weghorst is the striker." The video concludes with Manchester United players huddling together on the pitch, displaying a serious and focused demeanor, illuminated by stadium lights, while the vibrant, energetic atmosphere of the chanting crowd continues.

The minute opens with a wide-angle view of Wembley Stadium, packed with a vibrant crowd of fans in red and blue, as an announcer welcomes attendees to the high-stakes Emirates FA Cup match between Manchester City and Manchester United. The atmosphere is electric with crowd chants building anticipation. The camera pans across the pitch as players from both teams take their positions, and the referee's whistle signals the start of the match, met by a roar from the stadium crowd. Manchester City, in light blue jerseys, kick off from the center circle, and the commentator notes a "well-struck pass." Gameplay is fluid, with players like Mahrez (number 26) seen in action, moving the ball forward, as the commentator calls out his name. The action intensifies as Manchester City advances into the United penalty area. After a save attempt where the Manchester United goalkeeper is seen "flinging himself at it" according to the commentator, Haaland (number 9) scores past him. The scoreboard updates to MCI 1-0 MUN, and the crowd erupts in a massive celebration, amplified by the commentator exclaiming, "And a goal for Manchester City! They forge ahead!" The EA Sports Live broadcast overlay and Emirates FA Cup branding remain visible, underscoring the live event.

The minute opens with a close-up of Manchester City's Erling Haaland, in his light blue #9 jersey with 'Etihad Airways' branding, celebrating his goal with a clenched fist and intense expression, as a commentator remarks on the significance of this goal in the final. The camera follows him across the pitch before cutting to a wide view of Wembley Stadium, packed with fans in red and blue, where the scoreboard confirms 'MCI 1-0 MUN' at 03:28 amidst a strong, continuous crowd chant. A replay then shows Manchester City in light blue attacking Manchester United in red, with the ball near the United goal. A close-up captures the Manchester United goalkeeper in a blue patterned jersey diving in vain as the ball goes in, and Haaland is seen celebrating again, pointing to his ear, while a second commentator praises how Haaland "made the finish look easy" from a "decent delivery," calling it a "lovely goal." On-screen graphics confirm '9 Erling Haaland, GOAL 4'', followed by a brief FIFA 23 logo. Following a referee's whistle, the camera pulls back to a wide view of the pitch, showing teams repositioning for the restart at 04:52, with the commentator questioning if United can find a response now that they've been "shaken up." Gameplay continues with players like '17 DE BRUYNE', '8 FERNANDES', '9 HAALAND', and '21 ANFRE' moving the ball across the field, accompanied by the sounds of kicking and ongoing crowd murmurs and cheers. Pitch-side advertising boards display '#EmiratesFACup' and 'FIFA SOUND', with the EA Sports Live logo present, all contributing to the electric, high-stakes atmosphere of the Emirates FA Cup match under bright stadium lights.

event_blocks.summary: In the simulated Emirates FA Cup match, Manchester City holds a 1-0 lead over Manchester United under bright stadium lights, with the crowd creating an electric atmosphere. As Manchester City advanced from midfield, Commentator 1 identified Gundowan and Casemiro, noting a "lovely ball over the top" and exclaiming "Real chance!" leading to a massive crowd roar as Jack Grealish scored, celebrating by raising his arms and pointing to his ear, with the scoreboard updating and match time progressing from 06:35 to 12:00. Despite the goal, Commentator 1 remarked "no reward for them," with Commentator 2 adding "it was all good until the final effort," as player graphics identified Grealish, Haaland, De Bruyne, and Fernandes, and pitch-side boards displayed '#EmiratesFACup' and 'easports.com/fc', with the 'EA SPORTS LIVE' overlay visible. The referee reacted as the scene transitioned from wide gameplay to close-ups, emphasizing realism amidst chanting crowds. Later, with the score still 1-0 and time progressing from 12:05 to 18:03, Manchester United's "Marcus Rashford" was identified, and Manchester City players "Ake" and "Jack Grealish" advanced the ball, with a commentator noting Grealish "has options available." A "really good block" followed "Gundogan" being mentioned, and as Manchester City pushed, "Antony" was identified. A "good-looking ball in behind" led to a powerful shot by "Weghorst," met by a "great reflex action" from the Manchester United goalkeeper, described as a "brilliant save" that "should have been a goal," before the ball was cleared. Play then transitioned to Bruno Fernandes (number 8) preparing for a corner kick near the corner flag, with pitch-side advertising for 'Emirates FA Cup' and 'easports.com/fc' visible. The segment opened with a roaring crowd as Bruno Fernandes prepared to take a corner, with the scoreboard showing Manchester City leading 1-0 at 18:05. A referee's whistle signaled play, and a commentator noted Fernandes "favoring the short one." The camera shifted to a wide aerial view, and after the ball was cleared and play moved to midfield, commentators identified "Bruno Fernandes" and then "Fred," with an observation that "that's how they keep the opposition at bay." Manchester City advanced, and Erling Haaland received a pass, making a powerful run toward goal, prompting the commentator to shout "Haaland!" twice before exclaiming, "And a goal! To increase their lead in this final! Will they be stopped now?" Haaland scored at 21:05, sending the ball past the diving Manchester United goalkeeper, updating the scoreboard to 2-0, as the crowd erupted. Haaland celebrated by sliding on his knees, raising his arms, and pointing to his ear, with teammates congratulating him, and a replay showed the shot and the goalkeeper’s attempt. Another commentator analyzed Haaland's strength, balance, and "wonderful goal," as the predominantly blue crowd chanted and clapped, with pitch-side advertising displaying '#EmiratesFACup', 'FIFA SOUND', and 'easports.com/fc', and the EA Sports Live logo visible.

The segment opens with a roaring stadium crowd as Bruno Fernandes, in his red Manchester United #8 jersey with 'TeamViewer' branding and visible arm tattoos, prepares to take a corner kick near the white and yellow corner flag. The on-screen scoreboard shows Manchester City leading 1-0 at 18:05 in the Emirates FA Cup. A referee's whistle signals the start of play, and a commentator notes Fernandes "favoring the short one." The camera shifts to a wide aerial view as Manchester City players in light blue and Manchester United players in red reposition. event_blocks.summary: The simulated Emirates FA Cup match sees Manchester City leading Manchester United 2-1 as the action intensifies. Following a corner kick taken by Bruno Fernandes, Manchester City's defense clears the ball, narrowly missing a goal as the crossbar intervenes. Manchester City regains possession, their 'Ole, ole, ole' chants echoing through the stadium, while Casemiro of Manchester United reacts with a hand on his hip and Pep Guardiola animatedly directs a player in an orange bib. As Manchester City advances into the penalty area, David de Gea makes a crucial save, averting immediate danger. The play shifts to a counter-attacking possibility for Manchester United, but Manchester City retains control, moving effectively through midfield with Grealish and Haaland involved in the attack. The on-screen scoreboard updates to 34:59, displaying the score and competition details, accompanied by player graphics and pitch-side advertising, as Manchester City continues their offensive pressure.

Five minutes left in the second period of extra time here." The stadium crowd, a sea of red and blue, buzzes with continuous, loud chanting, creating an electric atmosphere. Players like Shaw (23), Akanji (25), Mahrez (26), Sabitzer (18), McTominay (39), Garnacho (49), and Phillips (4) are highlighted by on-screen graphics as they jostle for possession, with distinct ball kicking sounds punctuating the play. The Mini-Map dynamically updates, reflecting shifting formations. After a Manchester City throw-in, the commentator identifies "Bernardo Silva" as he attempts an attack, which "fizzles out." The referee in lime green remains central, overseeing restarts. As the clock hits 120:00, the commentator announces, "And the electronic board delivers the news, two minutes of stoppage time," intensifying the drama. Manchester United presses forward, while pitch-side advertising boards display 'Emirates FA Cup' and 'EA SPORTS LIVE' branding, all under the stadium lights and a clear sky, as City attempts to push on the front foot, with the commentator concluding, "And City on the front..."

event_blocks.summary: The Emirates FA Cup final concluded in a dramatic 5-5 draw after extra time, leading to a penalty shootout between Manchester City and Manchester United at Wembley Stadium. Following a tense shootout where both teams exchanged successful penalties, Manchester United goalkeeper David de Gea made a crucial save against Bernardo Silva. Antony then scored for United, followed by goals from Jack Grealish for City and Fred for United. Ederson equalized for City, but Fred scored again for United. Ultimately, Rashford scored the decisive penalty, securing a 6-5 victory for Manchester United. The stadium erupted in celebration for the jubilant United players, including Antony, Fred, Dalot, McTominay, Shaw, and Casemiro, who embraced and lifted the Emirates FA Cup Trophy under a 'WINNERS' arch amidst falling confetti and fireworks. Dejected Manchester City players were seen walking away from the pitch as commentators praised United's brilliant performance and the strong connection between the team and their fans.

raw_segments: The video segment opens with a wide aerial view of the Emirates FA Cup final, showing Manchester City in light blue and Manchester United in red, locked in a dramatic 5-5 draw at 120:00 of extra time. A commentator notes the teams are "still level here" with "no way through." A sharp whistle signals the end of extra time, and the commentator declares it a "fantastic final" that will end in the "most nerve-shredding way possible with a penalty shootout." Close-ups reveal dejected players, including a Manchester City player looking downcast and a Manchester United player touching his face in frustration, as both teams walk towards the center circle. An on-screen graphic confirms 'FULL TIME ET' and lists all goalscorers: Haaland (4', 22'), Mahrez (57'), Silva (86'), Grealish (105') for City, and Weghorst (67'), Rashford (25', 45', 83'), Antony (100') for United. Match statistics highlight Manchester United's 89% dribble success and 89% pass accuracy against City's 71% shot accuracy and 90% pass accuracy. As tense, upbeat background music plays, the penalty shootout commences, with the commentator announcing, "And so the penalty shootout about to commence." A penalty kick selection screen shows Mahrez and Sabitzer as top choices. The stadium crowd, a massive animated sea of blue and red, roars as Mahrez steps up for City. He scores, prompting the commentator to state, "He had to score, and he does." Sabitzer then takes his penalty for United, with the commentator observing, "And up he comes, doing his best to remain focused." He also scores, and the commentator confirms, "Job done from the spot." Álvarez scores for City, followed by Casemiro for United, updating the on-screen scoreboard to 1-1. The EA SPORTS LIVE overlay remains visible, alongside 'Emirates FA Cup' and 'EA SPORTS' branding on pitch-side advertising boards, as the intense shootout continues.

The dramatic climax of the Emirates FA Cup final penalty shootout unfolds with Manchester City's Bernardo Silva (#20) taking a penalty, which a commentator notes "wasn't the greatest" as Manchester United goalkeeper David de Gea, praised for reacting "magnificently," saves it. The on-screen scoreboard updates, reflecting the tension. Manchester United's Antony (#21) then steps up, and as a commentator exclaims "And it's gone in!", he scores, giving United the lead. Jack Grealish (#10) keeps City's hopes alive by scoring, followed by Fred (#17) who successfully converts his penalty, putting United ahead once more. The tension escalates as City's goalkeeper Ederson (#31) takes and scores his penalty, equalizing the shootout. Fred then scores again, and the camera cuts to ecstatic Manchester United fans in red, their cheers and whistles audible amidst the general stadium atmosphere. With the score tied 5-5 after regular time, a commentator anticipates the decisive moment, stating "if he scores, they win." A Manchester United player, later identified as Rashford (#10) in the full-time graphic, takes the final penalty and scores, sealing a 6-5 victory on penalties for United. The stadium erupts in joy, with loud cheers and celebrations from the predominantly red crowd, as Rashford raises his arms triumphantly. Commentators declare, "Oh, that's it! The final act in the drama. One team jubilant, the other distraught," reflecting on the "great entertainment" and acknowledging United's deserved win while noting City's disappointment. The EA SPORTS LIVE broadcast overlay remains visible, alongside 'Emirates FA Cup' and 'FIFA' branding on pitch-side advertising boards, as the video concludes with a full-time graphic displaying the final score and goal scorers, including Rashford's 100th-minute goal, and players embracing.

The segment opens with a close-up of a dejected Manchester City player, his head down, as the on-screen scoreboard confirms a dramatic 5-5 draw after extra time and a penalty shootout. Commentators immediately set the scene, noting that "their football was brilliant" and that "the party [is] in full swing now," anticipating "quite the night of celebration here" amidst a swelling crowd noise and celebratory music. The camera then shifts to the jubilant Manchester United players in red 'TeamViewer' jerseys, celebrating their hard-fought victory. Antony (#21), Fred (#17), and Dalot (#20) are seen high-fiving and embracing, their elation palpable as the team gathers in a huddle on the pitch, surrounded by photographers and staff, with confetti beginning to fall. Commentators emphasize this as "special for everybody at the club," a "great moment for any young player," and "what you dream about when you start playing football." The scene transitions to the winners' podium under a 'WINNERS' arch, where Manchester United players, including McTominay (#39), Casemiro (#18), and Fred (#17), walk towards the Emirates FA Cup Trophy. As commentators announce, "And now for the trophy lift itself," followed by "The cup winners!", the captain triumphantly raises the large silver cup with red 'Emirates Fly Better' ribbons high above his head, accompanied by a loud crowd roar and a cascade of confetti. The goalkeeper, in a blue patterned jersey, joins the celebration, raising his arms in victory. Wembley Stadium is packed with an animated crowd in red and blue, their energy palpable, as the EA SPORTS LIVE broadcast overlay remains visible, capturing this "fantastic stuff" and what "it means to those players and the manager."

The dramatic Emirates FA Cup final at Wembley Stadium culminates in a triumphant celebration for Manchester United, who emerged victorious after a 5-5 draw followed by a penalty shootout. Manchester United players, clad in their red 'TeamViewer' jerseys, gather under a large white 'WINNERS' arch as confetti rains down. Commentators reflect on their journey, noting, "my goodness, they've taken the rough with the smooth," and now "they savor the moment." Loud crowd cheers and a whistle punctuate the air as players like McTominay (#39), Shaw (#23), Fred (#17), and Dalot (#20) celebrate wildly, embracing and lifting the ornate Emirates FA Cup Trophy high above their heads. The goalkeeper, David de Gea, in his blue patterned jersey, joins the jubilation, raising his arms in victory. A commentator gives "full marks to the entire team," stating they now "get the chance to exhale and enjoy." The camera pans across the jubilant team forming a tight huddle, while in the background, the predominantly red stadium crowd erupts in cheers, with fireworks bursting in the night sky, adding to the grandeur. Commentators praise the team's "brilliant" performance and the fans who "really got behind their team," playing "a big part in this win," describing it as "a match made in heaven between players and fans." Dejected Manchester City players in their light blue kits are visibly walking away from the pitch. The EA SPORTS LIVE broadcast overlay remains visible, along with pitch-side advertising boards displaying '#EmiratesFACup' and 'EA SPORTS LIVE', as the commentators predict these celebrations "are going to continue for quite some time" amidst the ongoing confetti and fireworks.
